what: KRLS classification + non-gaussian response — research + plan
title: KRLS classification + non-gaussian response — research + plan
text: Status: deferred. LPM (linear-probability-model treatment 0/1 y) acceptable meantime — current behaviour, since krls() reject 0/1 y.

title: KRLS classification + non-gaussian response — research + plan
text: KRLS fundamentally L2 ridge regression RKHS: ĉ=(K+λI)−1y⇔minc∥y−Kc∥2+λc⊤Kc\hat{c} = (K + \lambda )^{-1} y \quad\Leftrightarrow\quad \min_c \|y - Kc\|^2 + \lambda c^\top K c binary y \\{0, 1\}, naive KRLS produces linear-probability- style fit — predictions can fall outside [0, 1], marginal effects probability scale (fine), noise model gaussian (wrong) SEs / CIs mis-calibrated, predict(..., type = "link") notion. poisson / gamma responses, KRLS gives mean-fit constrained positive, MLE-correct dispersion estimate, log-scale predictions.

title: KRLS classification + non-gaussian response — research + plan
text: Three established families fix:

what: A. Linear probability KRLS (do nothing different)
title: KRLS classification + non-gaussian response — research + plan
text: Hainmueller & Hazlett 2014 explicitly note KRLS--binary-y gives LPM-style fits, suitable marginal-effect interpretation probability prediction. bigKRLS KRLS::krls effectively — just reject 0/1 y. Pros: trivial; byte-equal current implementation user passes 0/1 y. Cons: predictions outside [0,1]; link function; pint PIs respect bounded scale.

what: B. Kernel logistic / GLM regression via IRLS
title: KRLS classification + non-gaussian response — research + plan
text: (Zhu & Hastie 2005, “Kernel Logistic Regression Import Vector Machine”; Wahba et al. 1995.) Replace squared loss deviance. Solve ĉ=arg⁡mincℓ(y,Kc)+λc⊤Kc\hat{c} = \arg\min_c \ell(y, Kc) + \lambda c^\top K c iteratively reweighted least squares: Newton step weighted ridge original kernel space. IRLS iter: given mu = link^{-1}(Kc), W = diag(d mu/d eta)^2 / Var(y|mu), z = Kc + (y - mu)/(d mu/d eta), solve (W^{1/2} K W^{1/2} + lambda ) (W^{-1/2} c_new) = W^{1/2} z. 5–15 iterations converge typically. Pros: principled MLE RKHS; correct deviance; correct SEs via Fisher information convergence; link-respecting predictions. Cons: weights break cached eigen structure iter — iter O(n^3) (re-eigendecompose W^{1/2} K W^{1/2}) use clever update. ~5x cost gaussian fit.

what: C. Post-hoc GLM on eigen features
title: KRLS classification + non-gaussian response — research + plan
text: (Mirrors ares’ family pattern; finite-rank kernel-GLM.) Eigendecompose K = V D V' (already done gaussian fit). Truncate k largest eigenvalues explain >= 99% variance (typically k \approx 50-200 n = 1000+). Treat truncated eigenvectors V_k (n x k) fixed feature matrix. Run stats::glm.fit(V_k, y, family = ...) k << n parameters -> well-posed, extra ridge needed inside GLM (eigtrunc ridge). Predictions new data X_new: build K_new (m x n), project m x k eigen-feature matrix X_new, multiply beta_glm -> linear predictor eta. Apply link^{-1} -> response-scale predictions. Pros: reuses gaussian-KRLS infrastructure entirely; one extra call glm.fit; fast (k x n vs n x n linear system); aligned ares’ “fit basis gaussian, post-hoc GLM” pattern. Cons: exact MLE min deviance + lambda c'Kc; MLE given truncated eigen-basis. k chosen sensibly indistinguishable full KLR solution precision users actually care (~1e-3 predictions).

title: KRLS classification + non-gaussian response — research + plan
text: Go C (post-hoc GLM eigen features), B stretch option users want strict KLR. Rationale: Mirrors ares pattern exactly. Syntax-alignment ares user’s stated goal last alignment chain. ares post-hoc- GLM--selected-basis. . Cheap. Adds one stats::glm.fit call per fit. new C++. re-eigendecomp per IRLS iter. new theory validate. Eigen-feature GLM standard reduced-rank GP-classification approximation; literature blesses . Marginal effects clean. Chain rule existing krls_deriv_cpp: d response / d x_k = d link^{-1}/d eta * d eta / d x_k. Single multiply. B future option. user demands true KLR (e.g. small-n, high-imbalance binary problem eigen-truncation crude), add family.method = c("eigen-glm", "irls"). , simpler better.

title: KRLS classification + non-gaussian response — research + plan
text: Mirror ares signature. Default family = "gaussian" (current behaviour). New families opt-.

title: KRLS classification + non-gaussian response — research + plan
text: Scope: Add family = c("gaussian", "binomial", "poisson", "gamma") arg krls.default (port validation block ares.default lines 286-360). Add family.eigtrunc.var = 0.99 arg (eigen-feature retention threshold; cap k <= n-1). binomial: validate y \{0, 1} 2-level factor / character / logical; coerce 0/1 numeric; stash factor levels predict roundtrip (ares). Run existing gaussian KRLS path centered y (y - mean(y)) get coefs / eigendecomp / sigma / lambda. gaussian fit converges, compute K %*% c -> gaussian-scale linear predictor. Refit step: take V_k (eigvectors cumulative variance >= 0.99), run stats::glm.fit(V_k, y, family = binomial()). Store beta_glm (length k). Replace $coeffs kernel-coefficient equivalent c_glm = V_k beta_glm (length n). makes K %*% c_glm = V_k beta_glm = eta_hat, linear predictor. Stash original gaussian solve $coeffs_gauss. Stash $family = "binomial", $y_levels, $family_fit = list(beta = beta_glm, k_used = k, V_k = V_k). fitted response-scale prediction: plogis(eta_hat). non-gaussian families, skip y-standardisation entirely. Add type = c("response", "link") arg (default "response"). Build K_new, compute eta_new = K_new %*% c_glm. type = "link": return eta_new. type = "response": return plogis(eta_new). se.fit: derived vcov(glm.fit) projected K_new V_k. Standard GLM delta method. 2-level factor responses, optional: caller passes factor y, predict returns factor type = "response" rounded — default behaviour return probabilities (matches predict.glm(type="response")). Currently krls_deriv_cpp returns d eta / d x_k (gaussian KRLS deriv). binomial: d p / d x_k = p (1 - p) . d eta / d x_k. binary = TRUE first-differences: replace per-pair predictions p(x_high) - p(x_low) response scale. Recovery: train logistic DGP y = 1{plogis(0.5*x1 + 0.5*x2) > U(0,1)}, n=500. Check AUC >= 0.85 holdout; calibration plot lines . vs glm(y ~ poly(x, 2), family = binomial) known nonlinear DGP — krls match beat AUC. vs ares family = "binomial" DGP — krls allowed differ ROC ballpark (within 0.02 AUC). Round-trip 2-level factor y -> factor predictions match input level encoding. predict(fit, type = "link") returns gaussian-scale eta; type = "response" returns probabilities [0, 1]. Scope estimate: ~200 LOC R, ~150 LOC tests. new C++.

title: KRLS classification + non-gaussian response — research + plan
text: Scope: Validation block (port ares.default lines 328-349): non-negative, integer-valued (tolerance). Fit pipeline Phase glm.fit(V_k, y, family = poisson()). $fitted = exp(eta_hat). predict(type = "response") = exp(K_new %*% c_glm), type = "link" = eta. Marginal effects: d lambda / d x_k = lambda . d eta / d x_k. binary first-differences: exp(eta(x_high)) - exp(eta(x_low)) rate scale. Tests: Recovery counts DGP y ~ Poisson(exp(0.5*x1 - 0.3*x2)), n=500. RMSE rate scale better glm(y ~ poly(x, 2), family = poisson). vs ares poisson DGP. Scope estimate: ~80 LOC R, ~80 LOC tests.

title: KRLS classification + non-gaussian response — research + plan
text: Phase B family = Gamma(link = "log"). Validation: y > 0 strict. Scope estimate: ~30 LOC R, ~50 LOC tests.

title: KRLS classification + non-gaussian response — research + plan
text: KRLS’ current interval = "pint" errors non-gaussian. Add: eta_lo, eta_hi = eta +/- z * se(eta) lo, hi = plogis(eta_lo), plogis(eta_hi) Wilson interval safer choice finite-sample binomial coverage near 0/1. lo, hi = exp(eta +/- z * se(eta)) gamma: poisson log scale. varmod non-gaussian: probably skip — GLM already dispersion baked . Document varmod applies gaussian fits. Tests: empirical coverage nominal 95% [0.92, 0.98] clean DGP per family. Scope estimate: ~60 LOC R, ~80 LOC tests.

what: Phase E — marginal effects on response scale (chain rule)
title: KRLS classification + non-gaussian response — research + plan
text: Currently derivatives avgderivatives computed eta scale. non-gaussian, users probably want response scale (interpretable “probability per unit x” binomial). Add marginal.scale = c("response", "link") arg, default "response" non-gaussian, "link" ignored gaussian. Multiply existing derivmat_s link-derivative evaluated row’s fitted mu. binomial: factor mu (1 - mu). poisson / gamma log link: factor mu. var.avgderivatives gets chain rule applied analytic variance derivation. Tests: known DGP, avgderivatives response scale close numerical-derivative--fitted; link scale raw gaussian-KRLS derivatives. Scope estimate: ~80 LOC R, ~80 LOC tests.

title: KRLS classification + non-gaussian response — research + plan
text: user needs multi-class: Add family = "multinomial" nnet::multinom Suggests dep. eigen-feature pattern: nnet::multinom(y ~ V_k - 1) k-class. Predictions: softmax K classes. Marginal effects: K x p matrix per observation. Scope: ~150 LOC R, ~120 LOC tests, adds nnet dep. Recommendation: ship round. Add user files issue.

title: KRLS classification + non-gaussian response — research + plan
text: -> B -> C -> D -> E -> F (deferred) Phase value driver (binomial users actually ask first). Phases B, C, D, E ride eigen-feature infrastructure builds mostly mechanical. Total scope -E ship: ~450 LOC R, ~440 LOC tests, 0 new C++, 0 new deps.

what: Open questions to resolve before kickoff
title: KRLS classification + non-gaussian response — research + plan
text: eigen-feature variance threshold default: 0.99 conservative (keeps many eigvecs). binary classification n=1000 clear signal, k=20 might plenty. expose family.k directly family.eigtrunc.var = 0.99 (relative threshold) — pick latter since auto-adapts spectrum. happens $coeffs non-gaussian fits? Option 1 (preferred): $coeffs always kernel- coefficient vector gives K %*% $coeffs = eta_hat. Add $coeffs_gauss original gaussian solve. way predict.krls_rr internally works families — just multiply K_new, apply link. Option 2: $coeffs undefined non-gaussian; $family_fit$beta meaningful. Worse — breaks K %*% coeffs interpretation users may rely . lambda.method = "cv" (Phase 3 last alignment) interact non-gaussian fits? gaussian eigen-fit picks lambda LOO MSE; binomial might want lambda chosen CV-deviance. Likely yes — extend Phase 3’s CV mechanism score deviance family non-gaussian. Small change, worth flagging. vcov non-gaussian: GLM produces vcov beta_glm. Project back via V_k: vcov(c_glm) = V_k vcov(beta_glm) V_k^T. Document asymptotic GLM covariance, gaussian-ridge sandwich.

title: ares: Fast MARS in roadrunner
text: ares() fits Multivariate Adaptive Regression Splines (MARS) models, flexible nonparametric regression technique introduced Friedman (1991). forward pass adds pairs hinge basis functions form max(0, ±(x - knot)), optionally multiplied parent term capture interactions. backward pass prunes terms minimising GCV criterion, K-fold CV criterion pmethod = "cv" nfold > 0. Beyond core MARS fitter, ares() supports hyperparameter autotuning, K-fold cross-validated pruning, bootstrap bagging, observation weights, binomial / poisson / gamma response families selected basis, residual-variance prediction intervals. vignette walks features turn.

title: ares: Fast MARS in roadrunner
code: library(roadrunner) fit <- ares(mpg ~ ., data = mtcars, nthreads = 2) print(fit) #> Call: #> ares.formula(x = mpg ~ ., data = mtcars, nthreads = 2) #>  #> MARS fit:  family = gaussian  #>   Selected terms: 6 of 21 forward-pass terms #>   RSS: 91.81  #>   GCV: 6.662  #>   Degree: 1   Penalty: 2   nthreads: 2 head(predict(fit)) #> [1] 19.36172 20.45321 25.35910 19.52265 17.68805 19.22091 head(predict(fit, mtcars[1:5, ])) #> [1] 19.36172 20.45321 25.35910 19.52265 17.68805

title: ares: Fast MARS in roadrunner
text: already use earth, gaussian-core fits ares() numerically close data, running multiple cores deterministically. chunk illustrates synthetic regression problem interaction quadratic component. two implementations typically agree training RSS within fraction percent, even choose slightly different knot sets.
code:
if (requireNamespace("earth", quietly = TRUE)) {   set.seed(20260509)   n <- 200; p <- 5   x <- matrix(runif(n * p), n, p)   y <- 10 * sin(pi * x[, 1] * x[, 2]) + 20 * (x[, 3] - 0.5)^2 +        10 * x[, 4] + 5 * x[, 5] + rnorm(n)   fa <- ares(x, y, degree = 2, nthreads = 2)   fe <- earth::earth(x, y, degree = 2)   cat("ares  RSS:", round(fa$rss, 3), " GCV:", round(fa$gcv, 3),       " nterms:", length(fa$selected.terms), "
")   cat("earth RSS:", round(fe$rss, 3), " GCV:", round(fe$gcv, 3),       " nterms:", length(fe$selected.terms), "
")   cat("relative RSS difference:",       round(abs(fa$rss - fe$rss) / fe$rss, 4), "
") } #> ares  RSS: 222.61  GCV: 1.707  nterms: 16  #> earth RSS: 204.18  GCV: 1.518  nterms: 15  #> relative RSS difference: 0.0903

title: ares: Fast MARS in roadrunner
text: core guarantee ares() varying nthreads change result. fixed seed.cv, fits bit--bit identical across thread counts, makes parallel execution safe reproduce.
code: set.seed(42) n <- 150; p <- 5 x <- matrix(runif(n * p), n, p) y <- 5 * pmax(0, x[, 1] - 0.5) * pmax(0, x[, 2] - 0.3) + rnorm(n) f1 <- ares(x, y, degree = 2, nthreads = 1) f2 <- ares(x, y, degree = 2, nthreads = 2) identical(f1$selected.terms, f2$selected.terms) #> [1] TRUE max(abs(f1$coefficients - f2$coefficients)) #> [1] 0

title: ares: Fast MARS in roadrunner
text: Setting autotune = TRUE runs inner K-fold cross-validated grid search (degree, penalty, nk, fast.k) refits winning combination full data. n large enough, ares() first tries grid small subsample short-circuit search one configuration wins clearly; typically cuts wall-clock time substantially accuracy loss.
code: set.seed(7) fit_at <- ares(mpg ~ ., data = mtcars, autotune = TRUE,                autotune.speed = "fast", seed.cv = 1L, nthreads = 2) fit_at$autotune$degree #> [1] 1 fit_at$autotune$penalty #> [1] 1 fit_at$autotune$nk #> [1] 42

title: ares: Fast MARS in roadrunner
text: binary outcomes, pass family = "binomial". Term selection runs numeric response (fast), selected basis refit binary response stats::glm.fit(). predict() returns probabilities default, linear predictor via type = "link".
code: set.seed(1) n <- 400 x <- matrix(rnorm(n * 4), n, 4) p <- plogis(0.5 * x[, 1] + 1.2 * x[, 2] * (x[, 2] > 0) - 0.4 * x[, 3]) y <- rbinom(n, 1, p) fb <- ares(x, y, family = "binomial", degree = 2, nthreads = 2) range(predict(fb)) #> [1] 0.2443712 0.9915275

title: ares: Fast MARS in roadrunner
text: gaussian fits, passing varmod = "const" varmod = "lm" stores residual variance model fit time. Subsequent calls predict(interval = "pint") return matrix columns c("fit", "lwr", "upr"). Use "const" homoscedastic data "lm" allow simple mean-dependent heteroscedasticity.

title: ares: Fast MARS in roadrunner
text: Friedman, J. H. (1991). Multivariate Adaptive Regression Splines. Annals Statistics 19(1):1–67.

code: @Manual{,   title = {roadrunner: Fast, Low-Dependency Machine Learning Algorithms},   author = {Jack T. Rametta},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.0.9041},   url = {https://cetialphafive.github.io/roadrunner/}, }

title: Fast, Low-Dependency Machine Learning Algorithms
text: Fast, low-dependency machine learning algorithms R. roadrunner ships C++ backed implementations classical ML algorithms thin, base-R-style interfaces. Two algorithms today: ares() – Multivariate Adaptive Regression Splines (MARS). krls() – Kernel Regularized Least Squares (KRLS).

title: Fast, Low-Dependency Machine Learning Algorithms
text: Low dependency. Rcpp, RcppArmadillo, RcppParallel runtime. tidyverse, rlang, S4. Fast. C++ engines via Rcpp multi-core scoring via RcppParallel. Deterministic. Fits bit--bit identical across thread counts fixed seed. Familiar API. Base-R style: formula matrix interfaces standard S3 methods (predict, print, summary, plot).

title: Fast, Low-Dependency Machine Learning Algorithms
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("CetiAlphaFive/roadrunner")

title: Fast, Low-Dependency Machine Learning Algorithms
text: See ares vignette autotune, classification, weights, bagging, prediction-interval examples.
code: library(roadrunner)  # Regression fit <- ares(mpg ~ ., data = mtcars, degree = 2) predict(fit, head(mtcars))  # Classification y <- as.integer(mtcars$am) fitc <- ares(as.matrix(mtcars[, -9]), y, family = "binomial") predict(fitc)  # Hands-free hyperparameter tuning fitt <- ares(mpg ~ ., data = mtcars, autotune = TRUE,              autotune.speed = "fast", seed.cv = 1L) fitt$autotune$degree

title: Fast, Low-Dependency Machine Learning Algorithms
text: krls() fits Kernel Regularized Least Squares model (Hainmueller Hazlett 2014) closed-form leave-one-selection ridge penalty per-observation marginal effects. krls() mirrors KRLS::krls() numerically (fits agree ~1e-13 matched sigma lambda) 6-10x faster benchmarks n >= 500.
code: set.seed(1) n <- 200 X <- matrix(rnorm(n * 3), n, 3) y <- sin(X[, 1]) + 0.5 * X[, 2]^2 - 0.3 * X[, 3] + rnorm(n, sd = 0.2)  fit <- krls(X, y)             # default sigma = ncol(X); lambda by LOO fit$avgderivatives            # average marginal effects per variable predict(fit, X)$fit           # in-sample fitted values  # Predict on new data with pointwise SEs Xnew <- matrix(rnorm(20 * 3), 20, 3) pr <- predict(fit, Xnew, se.fit = TRUE) head(pr$fit); head(pr$se.fit)

title: Fast, Low-Dependency Machine Learning Algorithms
text: Friedman, J. H. (1991). Multivariate Adaptive Regression Splines. Annals Statistics, 19(1):1-67. Hainmueller, J. Hazlett, C. (2014). Kernel Regularized Least Squares: Reducing Misspecification Bias Flexible Interpretable Machine Learning Approach. Political Analysis, 22(2):143-168.

what: Fast Multivariate Adaptive Regression Splines — ares
title: Fast Multivariate Adaptive Regression Splines — ares
text: Fits Multivariate Adaptive Regression Splines (MARS) model (Friedman 1991). forward pass adds pairs hinge basis functions form max(0, +/-(x - knot)); backward pass prunes terms minimising GCV criterion (K-fold CV criterion requested).

title: Fast Multivariate Adaptive Regression Splines — ares
code: ares(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'formula' ares(x, data = NULL, subset = NULL, ..., y = NULL)  # Default S3 method ares(   x,   y,   degree = 1L,   nk = NULL,   penalty = NULL,   thresh = 0.001,   minspan = 0L,   endspan = 0L,   adjust.endspan = 1L,   auto.linpreds = FALSE,   fast.k = 10L,   fast.beta = 1,   nprune = NULL,   pmethod = c("backward", "none", "cv"),   nfold = 0L,   ncross = 1L,   stratify = TRUE,   seed.cv = NULL,   cv.1se = FALSE,   autotune = FALSE,   autotune.speed = c("balanced", "quality", "fast"),   autotune.warmstart = TRUE,   n.boot = 0L,   na.action = c("impute", "omit"),   family = c("gaussian", "binomial", "poisson", "gamma"),   weights = NULL,   varmod = c("none", "const", "lm"),   trace = 0L,   nthreads = 0L,   ... )

title: Fast Multivariate Adaptive Regression Splines — ares
text: x numeric matrix data frame predictors, model formula calling formula method. ... Currently ignored. data data frame. Used formula method. subset Optional row-subsetting vector formula method (integer indices logical), passed stats::model.frame(). Ignored default (matrix/data-frame) method. y numeric response vector length nrow(x). family = "binomial", 0/1 numeric, logical, 2-level factor. degree Maximum interaction degree. Default 1 (additive). Use 2 3 two- three-way interactions. nk Maximum number basis terms forward pass. Default scales ncol(x): min(200, max(20, 2 * ncol(x))) + 1. penalty GCV penalty per knot. Default 2 degree = 1, 3 otherwise. Larger values produce sparser fits. thresh Forward-pass early-stop threshold relative RSS improvement. Default 0.001. minspan Minimum gap knots along variable. 0 (default) picks automatic value n p. endspan Distance data ends within knots forbidden. 0 (default) picks automatic value. adjust.endspan Multiplier applied endspan candidate hinge deepen existing interaction. Default 1 gaussian, poisson, gamma; 2 binomial. Pass explicit value override. auto.linpreds TRUE, hinge pairs whose best knot sits variable's range boundary replaced linear term. Default FALSE gaussian, poisson, gamma; TRUE binomial. Pass explicit value override. fast.k Size Fast-MARS candidate cache (Friedman 1993). Larger values rescore candidates step (slower, slightly accurate); 0 rescores every candidate every step. Default 10. fast.beta Age penalty stale entries Fast-MARS cache. Default 1.0. relevant fast.k > 0. nprune Maximum number terms backward pruning. Default nk. pmethod Pruning method. "backward" (default): minimise GCV backward-elimination subsets. "none": keep forward-pass terms. "cv": K-fold cross-validated subset selection. Requires nfold > 0 (sets nfold = 5 specified). nfold Number CV folds pmethod = "cv". Default 0 (CV; GCV-based pruning). nfold > 0, pmethod promoted "cv". ncross Number CV repetitions (builds fresh fold partition; per-size mean MSE averaged). Default 1. stratify TRUE (default), CV folds quantile-stratified y. Useful small n skewed responses. seed.cv Optional integer seed CV fold partition. Pass integer reproducible CV; NULL (default) uses current RNG state. cv.1se TRUE, applies one-standard-error rule pmethod = "cv": among sizes within one SE minimum mean CV-MSE, pick smallest. Default FALSE (pick argmin). autotune TRUE, runs inner cross-validated grid search (degree, penalty, nk, fast.k) refits winner full data. chosen settings full grid scores returned $autotune. Default FALSE. autotune.speed Speed/quality trade-autotune. used autotune = TRUE. "balanced" (default): explores moderate fast.k range picks smallest whose CV-MSE within 1% best. "quality": disables Fast-MARS cache (thorough, slowest). "fast": forces aggressive cache (cheapest, slight accuracy trade-). autotune.warmstart TRUE (default), autotune first tunes small subsample. one cell wins decisively, full grid skipped winner refit rows. applies autotune = TRUE n >= 200. n.boot Number bootstrap replicate fits bagging. Default 0 (bagging). > 0, predict() averages replicates plus central fit (se.fit = TRUE) returns per-prediction bag standard deviation. Composes autotune: replicate reuses central fit's chosen hyperparameters. na.action Strategy missing values x. "impute" (default) replaces column's NAs column's training median stores medians predict() reuse. "omit" drops rows NA. Either action warns. Missing values y, NaN/Inf anywhere x, always rejected. family Response family final coefficient fit. "gaussian" (default): identity link, OLS. "binomial": logit link. y may 0/1 numeric, logical, 2-level factor. "poisson": log link. y must non-negative integer-valued. "gamma": log link. y must strictly positive. non-gaussian families, term selection runs numeric y (fast); selected basis refit response scale via stats::glm.fit() requested family. weights Optional non-negative numeric vector length nrow(x) weighted least-squares fitting. Default NULL (unweighted). Term selection, GCV / CV pruning, autotune respect weights. Negative, NA, NaN weights rejected. varmod Residual variance model used predict() prediction intervals (gaussian ). "none" (default): variance model stored; interval = "pint" unavailable. "const": stores single residual SD; intervals yhat +/- qt(level, df) * sigma. "lm": fits small linear model |resid| yhat allow simple yhat-dependent heteroscedasticity. Captures residual scale changes linearly fitted mean. capture residual scale depends predictor whose contribution yhat small – case |resid| ~ yhat slope close zero "lm" collapses roughly "const". suspect x-driven heteroscedasticity (e.g. variance depends covariate orthogonal mean structure), varmod = "lm" help coverage degrade high-variance regions. Ignored non-gaussian families. trace Trace level. 0 (default) silent; 1 reports forward-pass progress. nthreads Number threads. 0 (default) uses RcppParallel::defaultNumThreads(). Examples vignette cap 2 CRAN compliance.

title: Fast Multivariate Adaptive Regression Splines — ares
text: object class "ares": list containing fitted coefficients, basis matrix bx, term directions dirs knots cuts, indices selected.terms, training rss gcv, fitted.values residuals, predictor names namesx, call, echoed control parameters. Non-gaussian fits additionally include $family, $glm (deviance, null.deviance, df.null, df.residual, aic, converged, iter), $linear.predictor; $fitted.values response scale (probabilities binomial, positive means poisson gamma). Autotune fits carry $autotune bagged fits carry $boot.

title: Fast Multivariate Adaptive Regression Splines — ares
text: Two interfaces provided. formula method takes model formula data frame. default method takes numeric predictor matrix x numeric response vector y. Fits deterministic across thread counts: fixed seed.cv, results bit--bit identical regardless nthreads.

title: Fast Multivariate Adaptive Regression Splines — ares
text: Friedman, J. H. (1991). Multivariate Adaptive Regression Splines. Annals Statistics 19(1):1-67. Friedman, J. H. (1993). Fast MARS. Stanford University Department Statistics Technical Report 110.

title: Fast Multivariate Adaptive Regression Splines — ares
code: fit <- ares(as.matrix(mtcars[, -1]), mtcars$mpg, nthreads = 2) print(fit) #> Call: #> ares.default(x = as.matrix(mtcars[, -1]), y = mtcars$mpg, nthreads = 2) #>  #> MARS fit:  family = gaussian  #>   Selected terms: 6 of 21 forward-pass terms #>   RSS: 91.81  #>   GCV: 6.662  #>   Degree: 1   Penalty: 2   nthreads: 2  p <- predict(fit, as.matrix(mtcars[, -1]))

title: Kernel Regularized Least Squares — krls
text: Fits Kernel Regularized Least Squares model Gaussian kernel, selecting ridge penalty leave-one-cross-validation via closed-form identity Hainmueller Hazlett (2014).  Marginal effects variances computed default returned original (X, y) scale.

title: Kernel Regularized Least Squares — krls
code: krls(X, ...)  # S3 method for class 'formula' krls(X, data = NULL, subset = NULL, ..., y = NULL)  # Default S3 method krls(   X,   y,   sigma = NULL,   lambda = NULL,   derivative = TRUE,   binary = TRUE,   vcov = TRUE,   weights = NULL,   L = NULL,   U = NULL,   tol = NULL,   eigtrunc = NULL,   lambda.method = c("loo", "cv"),   lambda.grid = NULL,   nfold = 0L,   ncross = NULL,   stratify = TRUE,   seed.cv = NULL,   cv.1se = FALSE,   autotune = FALSE,   autotune.grid = NULL,   varmod = c("none", "const"),   n.boot = 0L,   na.action = c("impute", "omit"),   trace = NULL,   nthreads = 0L,   print.level = NULL,   ... )  # S3 method for class 'krls_rr' predict(   object,   newdata = NULL,   se.fit = FALSE,   interval = c("none", "pint"),   level = 0.95,   ... )

title: Kernel Regularized Least Squares — krls
text: X numeric matrix data frame predictors (n x p). Constant columns rejected. Factor / character columns data frame expanded via model.matrix(~ ., x) (treatment contrasts, intercept dropped). Missing values handled via na.action (see ). ... Currently unused (caught forward compatibility). data Used formula method. data frame containing variables referenced formula. subset Used formula method. optional integer logical vector restricting rows data used fit. y numeric response vector single-column matrix.  Constant y rejected. sigma Gaussian-kernel bandwidth.  Default NULL, sets sigma via geomean_p formula: sqrt(median(d2) * p) d2 pairwise squared Euclidean distances standardised predictors p = ncol(X).  Must positive scalar supplied. lambda Optional ridge penalty.  NULL (default), selected golden-section search LOO error. derivative Logical.  TRUE (default), compute pointwise marginal effects average per variable.  Requires vcov. binary Logical.  TRUE (default), columns X exactly two unique values treated binary marginal effects replaced predicted-Y first differences (matches KRLS::fdskrls). vcov Logical.  TRUE (default), compute coefficient covariance variance average marginal effects. weights Optional vector observation weights (length n, strictly positive). Internally normalised mean 1. Implements weighted KRLS via D K D transform D = diag(sqrt(w)). weights = rep(1, n) byte-identical unweighted path. L, U Optional lower / upper bracket lambda search.  NULL, defaults follow KRLS::krls(). tol Tolerance lambda golden section.  Default 1e-6 (fixed, independent n; 1e-3 * n earlier versions). fixed small tolerance gives 6-digit lambda precision regardless sample size. eigtrunc Optional eigenvalue truncation cutoff (0, 1]. set, eigenvalues eigtrunc * max(d) dropped solve.  NULL (default) keeps eigenvalues. lambda.method Lambda-selection rule. "loo" (default) uses closed-form leave-one-golden-section search; "cv" uses K-fold CV grid (nfold > 0 required). lambda.grid Optional numeric vector lambda candidates lambda.method = "cv". NULL (default) auto-generates log-spaced grid [L, U]. nfold Number CV folds lambda.method = "cv" autotune. 0 (default) disables CV. ncross Number CV repetitions (builds fresh fold partition). Default NULL, resolves 2 autotune sigma search (repeated-CV stabilisation) 1 lambda.method = 'cv'. Explicit integer values honoured paths. stratify TRUE (default), CV folds quantile- stratified y. seed.cv Optional integer seed CV fold partition. cv.1se TRUE, applies one-standard-error rule picking lambda CV (smallest model within 1 SE minimum mean CV-MSE). Default FALSE. autotune TRUE, runs inner repeated-CV grid search sigma (default: 9-point multiplicative grid centred median-heuristic sigma anchor, anchor * 0.125 anchor * 32) refits winner full data using one-standard-error rule (selects largest sigma within 1 SE minimum CV-MSE). Default FALSE. autotune.grid Optional numeric vector sigma candidates autotune. NULL (default) uses 9-point anchor-centred grid sigma_anchor * c(0.125, 0.25, 0.5, 1, 2, 4, 8, 16, 32) sigma_anchor geomean_p anchor (sqrt(median(d2) * p) standardised predictors). varmod Residual variance model used construct prediction intervals via predict(..., interval = "pint"). "none" (default) disables PIs; "const" uses homoscedastic sigma_hat estimated training residuals. n.boot Number bootstrap replicates bagging. 0 (default) disables bagging. n.boot > 0, prediction averages across n.boot replicate fits. na.action handle missing values X. "impute" (default) replaces NAs column median (stored fit reapplied predict() time). "omit" drops rows NA. Missing y always error. trace Integer. 0 silent, > 0 enables progress diagnostics (currently: prints chosen lambda > 1, golden-section progress > 2). Replaces print.level. nthreads Integer. Number threads use C++ kernel build / decomposition. 0 (default) means use RcppParallel::defaultNumThreads(). print.level Deprecated alias trace. Prefer trace. object fitted "krls" object. newdata numeric matrix data frame columns training X (matching training formula).  NULL returns object$fitted. se.fit Logical.  TRUE, return pointwise standard errors predictions.  Requires fit created vcov = TRUE. interval Prediction-interval mode. "none" (default) returns point predictions ; "pint" returns lower/upper bounds confidence level level, requires fit created varmod = "const" (non-"none" varmod). level Confidence level interval = "pint". Default 0.95.

title: Kernel Regularized Least Squares — krls
text: object S3 class c("krls_rr", "krls") components mirroring KRLS::krls(): K, coeffs, Looe, fitted, X, y, sigma, lambda, R2, derivatives, avgderivatives, var.avgderivatives, vcov.c, vcov.fitted, binaryindicator. Formula-method fits additionally carry call, terms, xlevels, factor_info, na.action, na.medians downstream predict(), update(), model.matrix() support. Looe follows KRLS::krls() scale convention: sum squared leave-one-residuals standardised y scale multiplied sd(y), units [y^2 / sd_y] = [y].  preserved downstream compatibility code consumed KRLS::krls() output; LOO MSE raw-y squared units.

title: Kernel Regularized Least Squares — krls
text: Three call styles supported (mirrors ares()): krls(X, y, ...)  – matrix / numeric interface (back-compatible). krls(y ~ x1 + x2 + ..., data = df, ...) – formula interface factor expansion derived terms ((x^2), poly(x, 2), etc). krls(df, y, ...) df data frame numeric + factor / character columns – expands categorical columns via model.matrix(~ ., df) (treatment contrasts, intercept dropped). numerical pipeline mirrors KRLS::krls() exactly: X y standardised (column-centred, unit sd). Gaussian kernel K_ij = exp(-||x_i - x_j||^2 / sigma) built parallel C++. K eigendecomposed.  subsequent solves use eigen-basis closed forms, never inverting K + lambda directly. lambda selected golden-section search closed-form LOO error sum, (L, U, tol) bracket KRLS::krls. Marginal effects use closed-form identity dy/dx_k = -(2/sigma) * (X_k * (K c) - K diag(c) X)_k, computed without forming n x n distance matrix. Output unstandardised back original (X, y) scale. fixed (sigma, lambda), fits agree KRLS::krls() floating-point precision (typically < 1e-12 coefficients, fitted values, marginal effects n <= 1000).  Wall-clock time roughly 6-10x faster KRLS::krls() n >= 500 marginal effects variance estimates requested. Memory scales O(n^2): kernel squared eigenvector matrix stored.  Expect 0.4 * n^2 / 1e6 MB peak working memory (e.g. ~400MB n = 1000, ~10GB n = 5000). Scale-aware sigma default (geomean_p anchor, v0.0.0.9041) sigma = NULL, roadrunner sets Gaussian-kernel bandwidth using geomean_p formula: let d2_ij = ||Xs_i - Xs_j||^2 pairwise squared Euclidean distances standardised predictor matrix Xs; sigma = sqrt(median({d2_ij : < j}) * ncol(Xs)).  geometric mean raw median heuristic p = ncol(X), empirically lands sweet spot two extremes.  n=500, p=10 standardised N(0,1) data yields sigma ~ 13.5, versus ~ 18-19 raw median 10 KRLS::krls()'s fixed default. geomean_p: 15-DGP head--head vs KRLS::krls() (REQ-20260518-003, iter-2) geomean_p anchor shows roadrunner wins 12/15 DGPs, loses 0/15, ties 3/15.  raw median anchor (v0.0.0.9040) won 4/15 DGPs -smoothed locally nonlinear signals (exp-decay, interaction, tanh-interaction, poly2) selecting sigma ~2x wide.  geomean_p anchor preserves scale-awareness (adapts actual data distribution, unlike hard-coded sigma = p) avoiding -smoothing. n > 500 pairwise distance matrix O(n^2); keep default cheap, 500-row subsample drawn fixed seed (set.seed(2718)) result deterministic within session. Autotune-equals-default equivalence well-fit settings autotune enabled settings default sigma already near- optimal (e.g. additive interaction DGPs n=500, p=10), autotune grid centred sigma_anchor CV argmin coincides grid centre.  1-SE rule selects sigma_anchor, producing fit identical non-autotuned default.  expected behaviour — confirms default sigma well-chosen DGPs — failure autotune.  Autotune yields improvements optimal sigma departs anchor (e.g. sparse / linear DGPs much wider kernel better).

title: Kernel Regularized Least Squares — krls
text: fixed (sigma, lambda) fits remain byte-identical earlier versions roadrunner KRLS.  four defaults changed v0.0.0.9040 (sigma, tol, autotune nfold/ncross, autotune grid) affect results arguments left NULL / default.  Restore old default explicitly: sigma = ncol(X), tol = 1e-3 * nrow(X), nfold = 5, ncross = 1, autotune.grid = ncol(X) * c(0.25, 0.5, 1, 2, 4, 8). sigma anchor formula refined v0.0.0.9041 raw median (median(d2)) geomean_p formula (sqrt(median(d2) * p)).  restore v0.0.0.9040 median anchor, pass sigma = stats::median(.numeric(stats::dist(scale(X)))^2).

title: Kernel Regularized Least Squares — krls
text: Hainmueller, J. C. Hazlett (2014).  "Kernel Regularized Least Squares: Reducing Misspecification Bias Flexible Interpretable Machine Learning Approach."  Political Analysis 22(2):143–168.

title: Kernel Regularized Least Squares — krls
code: set.seed(1) n <- 100 X <- matrix(rnorm(n * 3), n, 3) colnames(X) <- c("age", "income", "score") y <- sin(X[, 1]) + 0.5 * X[, 2]^2 - 0.3 * X[, 3] +   rnorm(n, sd = 0.2)  fit <- krls(X, y) fit #> Kernel Regularized Least Squares (KRLS) #>   n = 100   p = 3  #>   sigma = 3.813   lambda = 0.222   R^2 = 0.9563  #>  #> Average Marginal Effects: #>        age     income      score  #>  0.6021180 -0.1008827 -0.2742915  fit$avgderivatives           # average marginal effect per variable #>           age     income      score #> [1,] 0.602118 -0.1008827 -0.2742915 summary(fit) #> Kernel Regularized Least Squares (KRLS) #>   n = 100  p = 3  sigma = 3.813  lambda = 0.222  R^2 = 0.9563 #>  #> Average Marginal Effects: #>         Estimate  Std. Err  t value  Pr(>|t|)     #> age     0.602118  0.022834  26.3690 < 2.2e-16 *** #> income -0.100883  0.021381  -4.7182 7.822e-06 *** #> score  -0.274292  0.020456 -13.4086 < 2.2e-16 *** #> --- #> Signif. codes:  0 ‘***’ 0.001 ‘**’ 0.01 ‘*’ 0.05 ‘.’ 0.1 ‘ ’ 1 #>  #> Quartiles of Pointwise Marginal Effects: #>           age     income      score #> 25% 0.3618781 -0.6924617 -0.3714887 #> 50% 0.7342455 -0.2581948 -0.3193604 #> 75% 0.8978449  0.5280357 -0.2050795  ## Formula interface with mixed numeric + factor predictors. df <- data.frame(age = X[, 1], income = X[, 2],                  region = factor(sample(c("N", "S"), n, replace = TRUE)),                  y = y) fit_f <- krls(y ~ age + income + region, data = df)  ## Predictions on new data with pointwise SEs. Xnew <- matrix(rnorm(20 * 3), 20, 3) colnames(Xnew) <- colnames(X) pr <- predict(fit, Xnew, se.fit = TRUE) head(pr$fit) #>             [,1] #> [1,]  1.62457968 #> [2,]  0.01886781 #> [3,] -0.36472318 #> [4,] -0.94094352 #> [5,]  0.09923114 #> [6,]  1.15424173 head(pr$se.fit) #>            [,1] #> [1,] 0.08130310 #> [2,] 0.11459798 #> [3,] 0.07798571 #> [4,] 0.09297983 #> [5,] 0.11479828 #> [6,] 0.09957523

what: Diagnostic plots for an ares fit — plot.ares
title: Diagnostic plots for an ares fit — plot.ares
text: Six diagnostic panels modelled stats::plot.lm(): residuals vs fitted, normal Q-Q standardized residuals, scale-location, Cook's distance, residuals vs leverage (Cook's-distance contours), Cook's distance vs leverage. default draws panels 1, 2, 3, 5 2x2 grid device single-panel layout; matches plot.lm() default. non-gaussian families (binomial, poisson, gamma), deviance residuals used panel 1 IRLS working weights used compute leverages standardized residuals (GLM analogue gaussian hat matrix).

title: Diagnostic plots for an ares fit — plot.ares
code: # S3 method for class 'ares' plot(   x,   which = c(1L, 2L, 3L, 5L),   caption = list("Residuals vs Fitted", "Normal Q-Q", "Scale-Location",     "Cook's distance", "Residuals vs Leverage", expression("Cook's dist vs Leverage  " *     h[ii]/(1 - h[ii]))),   panel = if (add.smooth) graphics::panel.smooth else graphics::points,   sub.caption = NULL,   main = "",   ask = prod(graphics::par("mfcol")) < length(which) && grDevices::dev.interactive(),   ...,   id.n = 3L,   labels.id = NULL,   cex.id = 0.75,   qqline = TRUE,   cook.levels = c(0.5, 1),   add.smooth = getOption("add.smooth", TRUE),   label.pos = c(4, 2) )

title: Diagnostic plots for an ares fit — plot.ares
text: x fitted object class "ares". Integer subset 1:6 selecting panels draw. Default c(1, 2, 3, 5) (plot.lm() default). caption Length-6 list panel captions; defaults match plot.lm(). panel Panel function scatter panels; defaults graphics::panel.smooth(). sub.caption Optional caption shown outer margin (one per page). Defaults deparsed x$call. main Title individual panels (typically ""). ask TRUE, prompt page multiple panels drawn single-panel device. ... graphical parameters passed underlying plot() calls. id.n Number extreme points label per panel (default 3; set 0 suppress). labels.id Character vector row labels; defaults 1:n. cex.id cex point labels. qqline Logical; TRUE, add Q-Q reference line panel 2. cook.levels Cook's-distance contours drawn panel 5. add.smooth Logical; passed panel. label.pos pos argument forwarded graphics::text().

title: Diagnostic plot for KRLS fits — plot.krls_rr
text: Invisibly returns x.

title: Diagnostic plot for KRLS fits — plot.krls_rr
text: Leverage uses KRLS hat matrix $$H = K (K + \lambda )^{-1} = V \mathrm{diag}(d/(d+\lambda)) V'$$ h_i = sum_k V[,k]^2 * d_k / (d_k + lambda). effective degrees freedom sum(d/(d+lambda)). Residual dispersion sqrt(RSS / (n - effdf)) ($varmod$sigma_hat present).

what: Predictions from an ares fit — predict.ares
title: Predictions from an ares fit — predict.ares
text: Returns predictions new data fitted ares MARS model. bagged fits (n.boot > 0), returns mean across central fit bootstrap replicates; se.fit = TRUE, per-row bag standard deviation attached "sd" attribute. gaussian fits built residual variance model (varmod = "const" "lm"), interval = "pint" returns prediction intervals.

title: Predictions from an ares fit — predict.ares
code: # S3 method for class 'ares' predict(   object,   newdata = NULL,   type = c("response", "link"),   se.fit = FALSE,   interval = c("none", "pint"),   level = 0.95,   ... )

title: Predictions from an ares fit — predict.ares
text: object object class "ares". newdata numeric matrix data frame new predictors. NULL (default) returns object$fitted.values. type Prediction scale. "response" (default): probabilities binomial, response-scale means poisson gamma, fitted values gaussian. "link": linear predictor. Coincides "response" gaussian fits. se.fit TRUE fit built n.boot > 0, returned vector carries "sd" attribute holding per-row bag standard deviation. Default FALSE. interval Type interval return. "none" (default): return point predictions . "pint": return matrix columns c("fit", "lwr", "upr") using variance model stored fit time. Requires family = "gaussian" varmod = "const" "lm"; errors otherwise. level Confidence level prediction intervals interval = "pint". Default 0.95. ... Currently unused.

title: Predictions from an ares fit — predict.ares
text: numeric vector predictions, matrix columns c("fit", "lwr", "upr") interval = "pint". se.fit = TRUE bagged fit, vector carries "sd" attribute.

title: Predictions from an ares fit — predict.ares
code: fit <- ares(as.matrix(mtcars[, -1]), mtcars$mpg, nthreads = 2) p <- predict(fit, as.matrix(mtcars[, -1]))

what: Print method for ares fits — print.ares
title: Print method for ares fits — print.ares
text: Prints call, family, term count, training RSS GCV, control parameters used. family = "binomial", also prints deviance, McFadden pseudo-R^2, AIC, IRLS convergence.

what: roadrunner: Fast, Low-Dependency Machine Learning Algorithms — roadrunner-package
title: roadrunner: Fast, Low-Dependency Machine Learning Algorithms — roadrunner-package
text: roadrunner collection fast, low-dependency implementations classical machine learning algorithms thin, base-R-style interfaces. two algorithms shipped today :

title: roadrunner: Fast, Low-Dependency Machine Learning Algorithms — roadrunner-package
text: ares() – Multivariate Adaptive Regression Splines (MARS) GCV / cross-validated pruning, hyperparameter autotune, bagging, weights, binomial / poisson / gamma families, prediction intervals. Built Friedman's (1991) fast least-squares forward selection parallelised knot search. krls() – Kernel Regularized Least Squares (Hainmueller Hazlett 2014) closed-form leave-one-lambda selection, per-observation marginal effects, binary first-difference handling. engines C++ top Rcpp, RcppArmadillo, RcppParallel. Fits deterministic across thread counts fixed seed.

title: roadrunner: Fast, Low-Dependency Machine Learning Algorithms — roadrunner-package
text: Friedman, J. H. (1991). Multivariate Adaptive Regression Splines. Annals Statistics, 19(1):1-67. Hainmueller, J. Hazlett, C. (2014). Kernel Regularized Least Squares: Reducing Misspecification Bias Flexible Interpretable Machine Learning Approach. Political Analysis, 22(2):143-168.

title: roadrunner: Fast, Low-Dependency Machine Learning Algorithms — roadrunner-package
text: Maintainer: Jack T. Rametta jtrametta@gmail.com (ORCID) Authors: Jack T. Rametta jtrametta@gmail.com (ORCID)

what: krls() — scale-aware sigma anchor refined to geomean_p (REQ-20260518-003)
title: roadrunner 0.0.0.9041
text: default sigma anchor formula .krls_sigma_anchor() updated raw median heuristic (median(d2), introduced v0.0.0.9040) geomean_p formula: sqrt(median(d2) * p) d2 pairwise squared Euclidean distances standardised training matrix p = ncol(X). : 15-DGP head--head vs KRLS::krls() n=500, p=10 (iter-0, REQ-20260518-003) showed v0.0.0.9040 median anchor -smoothed locally nonlinear signals, producing 8 losses 0 ties favour KRLS. anchor sweep 6 candidate formulae (iter-1) identified geomean_p clear winner (14/15 DGPs). Verification patched anchor (iter-2) confirmed: roadrunner wins 12/15 DGPs, KRLS wins 0/15, 3 ties. Delta table — iter-0 (median) vs iter-2 (geomean_p) mean MSE ratio (rr / KRLS): n=500, p=10 standardised N(0,1) data geomean_p anchor gives sigma ~ 13.5, splitting difference raw median (~18–19) KRLS’s fixed sigma = p = 10. formula still data-adaptive: non-standardised non-unit-variance inputs differ sensibly extremes. one-line R change (R/krls.R, .krls_sigma_anchor()); C++ engine logic unchanged. fixed (sigma, lambda) fits remain byte-identical prior versions. restore v0.0.0.9040 median anchor, pass sigma = stats::median(.numeric(stats::dist(scale(X)))^2).

title: roadrunner 0.0.0.9040
text: Addresses overfitting confirmed diagnostic sweeps REQ-20260518-001. four changes R-level ; C++ engine unchanged, fixed (sigma, lambda) fits remain byte-identical earlier versions. Empirical improvement signal DGPs (n=500, p=10, tune=none, R=10 replications; overfit ratio = test_MSE / train_MSE): improvement attributable Fix 1 (sigma default) Fix 2 (lambda tolerance); Fixes 3 4 stabilise autotune sigma selection. Breaking defaults (old values restorable explicit arguments): Default sigma changed ncol(X) median pairwise squared Euclidean distance standardised predictors (‘median heuristic’). n=500, p=10 oracle sigma ~20 median heuristic anchors near neighbourhood; sigma = ncol(X) 10 (2x -smoothed). Restore old behaviour sigma = ncol(X). Default lambda tolerance changed 1e-3 * n (n-dependent, coarse moderate n) 1e-6 (fixed, 6-digit precision). LOO golden-section empirically selecting lambda ~4x argmin sigma=20, n=500 old tolerance. L-bracket climb now uses multiplicative steps (x10 per step) instead additive steps (0.05 per step) scale-robustness. Restore old behaviour tol = 1e-3 * nrow(X). Autotune sigma grid changed ncol(X) * c(0.25, 0.5, 1, 2, 4, 8) (6 points fixed d) sigma_anchor * c(0.125, 0.25, 0.5, 1, 2, 4, 8, 16, 32) (9 points centred median heuristic anchor). Restore old behaviour autotune.grid = ncol(X) * c(0.25, 0.5, 1, 2, 4, 8). Autotune CV stabilised: default folds raised 5 10; default cross-partition repeats raised 1 2 (via ncross argument, whose default changes 1L NULL); sigma selection now applies 1-SE rule (largest sigma within 1 SE minimum CV-MSE, biasing toward wider kernels evidence weak). autotune component fit gains new fields: ncross, mse_per_fold, se_mse, cv.1se, sigma_1se. Restore old fold count nfold = 5; restore old repeat count ncross = 1.

what: New feature: krls() – Kernel Regularized Least Squares
title: roadrunner 0.0.0.9033
text: Adds krls(), -scratch implementation Hainmueller Hazlett (2014) KRLS estimator roadrunner roof. algorithm mirrors KRLS::krls() exactly (standardisation, Gaussian kernel, eigen-basis closed-form solve, golden-section LOO lambda search, marginal effects, binary first-difference handling); matched (sigma, lambda) fits agree KRLS::krls() within floating-point precision (< 1e-12 coefficients, fitted values, pointwise average marginal effects, prediction SEs). Engine C++ (src/krls.cpp) top RcppArmadillo (added LinkingTo + Imports) RcppParallel. Gaussian kernel test-vs-train kernel built parallel TBB worker. Eigendecomposition dispatched LAPACK via arma::eig_sym divide--conquer driver. Marginal effects computed via identity dy/dx_k = -(2/sigma)*(X_k*(K c) - K diag(c) X)_k, avoids explicit n x n distance matrix reduces average- marginal-effect variance O(n^3) O(n^2) per variable via row-sum trick. Measured speed-KRLS::krls() simulated benchmarks (derivative = TRUE, vcov = TRUE) nthreads = default: n=200 p=3 ~2x; n=500 p=3 ~5-6x; n=500 p=10 ~10x; n=1000 p=3 ~6x; n=1000 p=10 ~10x. Coefficient max-abs error vs KRLS::krls() < 2e-13 every cell. S3 class c("krls_rr", "krls") predict(), print(), summary() dispatch unambiguously roadrunner methods even KRLS loaded session. inherits(fit, "krls") preserved downstream-compat checks. Tests: tests/testthat/test-krls.R (parity vs KRLS::krls coefficients, fitted values, Looe, marginal effects, prediction SEs, binary first-differences; structural input-validation; predict recovers fitted values; sensible R^2). 11 tests, 28 assertions, green.

title: roadrunner 0.0.0.9032
text: BUG-013 (usability, low): print.ares() summary.ares() used silent bagging (n.boot > 0) autotune state, bagged autotuned fit printed identically plain fit. Fix: add one-line Bagging: n.boot = N replicate(s) Autotune: degree=D penalty=P nk=K fast.k=F warmstart=T/F blocks printers corresponding components present. summary.ares also carries $boot $autotune print method. Regression test: tests/testthat/test-bug-013-print-bag-autotune.R. BUG-011 (correctness, medium): ares.formula() silently dropped subset = ... (fell ... went nowhere) silently absorbed offset(...) terms ordinary predictors. produced wrong fits zero indication. Fix: () add explicit subset arg ares.formula pass model.frame via lm()-style match.call() construction (NSE inside model.frame doesn’t trip subset symbol resolving base R function); (b) detect offset() terms via attr(terms, "offset") model.frame runs stop() actionable message. Offset pass-post-hoc GLM refit punted later release (touches predict + bag + autotune compose paths). Regression test: tests/testthat/test-bug-011-formula-subset-offset.R. BUG-008 (correctness, high): predict() used return finite WRONG values newdata rows containing NA training used na.action = "omit". pre-existing warning promised “affected rows return NA predictions” code never imposed NA – NaN > 0 C++ hinge evaluates FALSE, collapsing affected hinge 0 yielding deterministic wrong prediction. Fix: detect NA rows xnew C++ basis pass, zero-fill NaN cells, re-impose NA rows linear-predictor compute (including bag mean, bag SE, interval = "pint" matrix path). Regression test: tests/testthat/test-bug-008-predict-na-rows.R. BUG-009 (correctness, high): bagged predict(..., type = "link") non-gaussian families used return g(mean(g^{-1}(eta_b))), .e. link applied response-scale bag mean. Jensen’s inequality mean(eta_b), simulator audit showed divergences hundreds log-odds units binomial bags moderate signal – making type = "link" numerically unreliable downstream use. Fix: collect per-replicate linear predictors etas response-scale predictions resps separately; type = "link" returns rowMeans(etas), type = "response" returns rowMeans(resps) (unchanged prior behaviour). Bag SE computed whichever scale returned. Regression test: tests/testthat/test-bug-009-bagged-link-jensen.R. BUG-012 (correctness, medium): formula-path fits derived terms ((x^2), poly(x, 2), log(x + 10), scale(x), splines::bs(x), …) used fit successfully predict() fail “newdata missing columns: (x^2)” predict.ares looked expanded column name literal column newdata, re-evaluating original terms object newdata. Fix: object$terms non-null (formula path), use model.matrix(delete.response(terms), newdata, xlev = object$xlevels) rebuild design derived terms re-evaluated. Falls back prior column-lookup path terms object stored (matrix interface). ares.formula now also stashes xlevels fit. Regression test: tests/testthat/test-bug-012-predict-derived-terms.R. BUG-010 (robustness, medium): sister BUG-004. NA values factor / character newdata columns used fall OOV detector (handled non-NA character values), model.matrix(~ ., newdata)’s default na.action = na.omit silently dropped rows – length(predict(fit, newdata)) less nrow(newdata). Fix: detect NA factor / character newdata columns front; error clear message naming column(s), mirroring BUG-004’s OOV path. Regression test: tests/testthat/test-bug-010-predict-factor-na.R.

title: roadrunner 0.0.0.9031
text: Autotune now reuses Householder R / Qty computed shared forward pass instead recomputing inside mars_backward_only_cpp call. Saves O(n*M^2) initial Householder pass every per-cell backward replay; cached R / Qty byte-identical recomputed values, selected basis, coefficients, GCV unchanged. Measured speedup v0.26 speed baseline (inst/sims/v0.26-speed-baseline.R, 24-cell grid x 5 reps, nthreads=4): geometric-mean 1.23x across grid; autotune cells specifically 1.05-1.15x (gaussian highdim p=20: 1.09x; gamma n=1000 p=10: 1.11x; binomial n=1500 p=20: 1.15x). Determinism invariant (nthreads=1 == nthreads=N) preserved. Internal C++ entries gain optional compute_forward_qr (mars_fit_cpp) R_in / Qty_in (mars_backward_only_cpp) parameters; public R API unchanged.

title: roadrunner 0.0.0.9030
text: plot(fit) now produces 4-panel diagnostic display 2x2 grid, modelled stats::plot.lm(): residuals vs fitted, normal Q-Q standardized residuals, scale-location, residuals vs leverage Cook’s-distance contours. Panels 4 (Cook’s distance) 6 (Cook’s vs leverage) also available via . binomial / poisson / gamma fits, residual-vs-fitted panel uses deviance residuals hat matrix uses canonical-link IRLS working weights. training observation weights now stored fitted object ($weights).

title: roadrunner 0.0.0.9029
text: Triage 2026-05-11 adversarial audit (audit-2026-05-11/): BUG-001 (high): bagged GLM refits family = "binomial" | "poisson" | "gamma" now reuse bootstrap indices used select basis. unseeded path used redraw indices live RNG, post-hoc GLM coefficients fitted different bootstrap sample basis. BUG-002: predict(bagged_fit) (newdata = NULL) now returns bag mean, matching predict(bagged_fit, x_train). training x stored fit ($x) support . BUG-003: varmod = "lm" prediction intervals now warn floor meaningful lower bound (rather 1e-12) extrapolation makes predicted MAD non-positive. -sample PIs unchanged. BUG-004: predict() errors loudly --vocabulary factor character levels newdata, instead silently dropping affected rows. BUG-005: weights must now strictly positive. Zero-weight rows used bias GCV downward produce -fitting; drop rows x/y instead. BUG-006: documented honestly varmod = "lm" captures yhat-dependent residual scale, x-driven heteroscedasticity. BUG-007: family = "poisson" rejects (y == 0) (degenerate GLM fit); families reject constant y (intercept can fit).

title: roadrunner 0.0.0.9028
text: Renamed ares roadrunner. MARS fitter remains available ares(), "ares" S3 class methods unchanged. Update library(ares) calls library(roadrunner).

title: roadrunner 0.0.0.9028
text: family accepts "gaussian" (default), "binomial", "poisson", "gamma". forward + backward MARS pass runs numeric response; selected basis refit stats::glm.fit() non-gaussian families. predict() gains type = c("response", "link"). weights argument observation weights. Composes CV pruning, autotune, bagging. pmethod = "cv" (convenience trigger nfold > 0) performs K-fold cross-validated subset-size selection. Optional ncross repetitions, quantile-based stratification, 1-SE rule via cv.1se = TRUE. autotune = TRUE runs inner-CV grid search (degree, penalty, nk, fast.k). autotune.speed chooses among "balanced" (default), "quality", "fast". 20 % subsample warm-start short-circuits full grid best-per-degree gap decisive. n.boot > 0 fits row-bootstrap replicates central model. predict() averages across replicates; se.fit = TRUE attaches per-row bag standard deviation. varmod = "const" | "lm" (gaussian ) stores residual variance model fit time, enabling predict(interval = "pint") approximate prediction intervals. na.action = c("impute", "omit") handles missing values x. Default "impute" median-imputes numeric columns stores medians reapplication predict time. Factor character columns data-frame x expanded via model.matrix replayed new data.

title: roadrunner 0.0.0.9028
text: Fits parallel-deterministic: fixed seed.cv, results byte-identical across thread counts. Default auto.linpreds = TRUE adjust.endspan = 2L family = "binomial" (matches earth’s binomial defaults). families keep gaussian-conservative defaults.

title: roadrunner 0.0.0.9028
text: Requires R (>= 4.1.0). Depends Rcpp RcppParallel. earth, bench, testthat Suggests .
